message:
mcp_tools: import CompatRuntime{,Builder} directly from runtime_compat instead of re-exporting through mcp

After mcp.rs stopped re-exporting `CompatRuntime` /
`CompatRuntimeBuilder` (they now live directly in `runtime_compat`
and are imported unconditionally at the mcp.rs top-level), the
`use super::{..., CompatRuntime, CompatRuntimeBuilder, ...}` line in
`mcp_tools.rs` was referring to symbols that no longer exist on the
`super::` (i.e. `crate::mcp`) path on tokio-only builds.

Fix:

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
  - Add `#[cfg(test)] use crate::runtime_compat::{CompatRuntime,
    RuntimeBuilder as CompatRuntimeBuilder};` at the top of the
    file, next to the other `crate::`-scoped imports. The
    `#[cfg(test)]` gate matches how the rest of `mcp_tools.rs` uses
    these types — only the test module (not production code)
    instantiates a runtime for synchronous scaffolding.
  - Remove `CompatRuntime, CompatRuntimeBuilder,` from the
    `super::{...}` import list and let rustfmt re-flow the remaining
    imports. No other symbols in that block change.

No behavior change; this is purely a fix for a dangling re-export
after the mcp.rs reorganization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
